findings.title: Hard-coded Credentials
findings.description: Hard-coded password found in source code
findings.fix: Use environment variables or secure credential management

findings.title: Hard-coded API Key
findings.description: Hard-coded API key exposed in source code
findings.fix: Store API keys in secure configuration or environment variables

input.code:
query = "SELECT * FROM users WHERE id = " + user_id
cursor.execute(query)

findings.title: SQL Injection Vulnerability
findings.description: Dynamic SQL query construction without parameterization allows SQL injection
findings.fix: Use parameterized queries or prepared statements

findings.title: Command Injection Vulnerability
findings.description: User input directly passed to system command execution
findings.fix: Validate and sanitize input, use subprocess with shell=False

findings.title: Cross-Site Scripting (XSS)
findings.description: User input rendered without HTML escaping
findings.fix: Use proper HTML escaping or template engine with auto-escaping

findings.title: Path Traversal Vulnerability
findings.description: Unvalidated file path allows directory traversal attacks
findings.fix: Validate file paths and use os.path.join with proper bounds checking

findings.title: Use of Weak Cryptographic Algorithm
findings.description: MD5 is cryptographically broken for password hashing
findings.fix: Use bcrypt, scrypt, or Argon2 for password hashing

findings.title: Insecure Deserialization
findings.description: Pickle deserialization of untrusted data can lead to code execution
findings.fix: Use safe serialization formats like JSON or validate data before deserializing

input.code:
import requests
url = request.args.get('url')
response = requests.get(url)

findings.title: Server-Side Request Forgery (SSRF)
findings.description: Unvalidated URL parameter allows SSRF attacks
